title: Security Engineering Inside AI Pipelines
modules: Module 1: Where AI Pipelines Break — Supply Chain, Training Data, and Deployment Threats
Module 2: Threat Modeling for ML Systems — STRIDE Applied to AI Workflows
Module 3: Securing Data Ingestion, Labeling, and Model Training Environments
Module 4: Vulnerability Scanning and SAST for AI-Adjacent Code and Dependencies
Module 5: Model Signing, Provenance Tracking, and Artifact Integrity
Module 6: Runtime Monitoring — Detecting Inference Attacks and Model Drift in Production
Module 7: Building Security Gates into CI/CD Pipelines for AI Releases
synopsis: A compromised model training pipeline can silently corrupt an AI system's behavior at scale — and most DevSecOps practices were designed for traditional software, leaving critical gaps. This course teaches security engineers and developers exactly where AI-specific attack surfaces emerge in the development lifecycle and how to close them.
rationale: As organizations ship AI features through automated pipelines, attackers are targeting training data, model registries, and inference endpoints that fall outside conventional AppSec scope.
learning_outcome: Learners can threat model an AI pipeline, identify its highest-risk components, and implement concrete security gates from data ingestion through production deployment.
source_signals: NIST Releases New Control Overlays to Manage Cybersecurity Risks in AI Systems
